zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel le Despenser於1312年出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾格盧斯特時，她的父親Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser26歲，母親Eleanor de Clare20歲。她於1321年2月9日與Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundel在英國艾可哈維林-亞特-包維爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1356年在英國格盧斯特斯爾享年44歲，埋葬在英國格盧斯特斯爾福可斯波利福可斯波利 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel le Despenser was born in 1312, in Gloucester, Gloucestershire, England, her father, Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser, was 26 and her mother, Eleanor de Clare, was 20. She married Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundel on 9 February 1321, in Havering-atte-Bower, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1356, in Gloucestershire, England, at the age of 44, and was buried in Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel le Despenser est née en 1312, à Gloucester, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Eleanor de Clare, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundel le 9 février 1321, à Havering-atte-Bower, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1356, à Gloucestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 44 ans, et a été enterrée à Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel le Despenser 1312 in Gloucester, Gloucestershire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser 26 und ihre Mutter Eleanor de Clare 20. Sie hat am 9. Februar 1321 in Havering-atte-Bower, Essex, England Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundel geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1356 im Alter von 44 Jahren in Gloucestershire, England und wurde in Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel le Despenser è nata il nel 1312 nel Gloucester, Gloucestershire, England, sua padre, Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Eleanor de Clare, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundel il 9 febbraio 1321, nel Havering-atte-Bower, Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1356, nel Gloucestershire, England, all'età di 44 anni, e fu sepolta a Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel le Despenserが1312年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアグロウセスターで生まれた時，父Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenserは26歳，母Eleanor de Clareは20歳であった。1321年2月9日にイギリスエセックスヘイバーイング-アッテ-ボウエルでSir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundelと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1356年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアで44歳で死去し，イギリスグロウセステルシュアイアテウケスブリーテウケスブリー アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel le Despenser(이)가 1312년 영국 그루스스터실르 그루스스터에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Eleanor de Clare(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1321년 2월 9일 영국 에세스 하버링-엇-보워에서 Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundel(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1356년 영국 그루스스터실르에서 44세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 그루스스터실르 튜케스버이 튜케스버이 애브브이에 묻혔다.

mn.Westminster, London, England: Westminster, London, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel le Despenser nasceu em 1312, em Gloucester, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Eleanor de Clare, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundel em 9 de fevereiro de 1321, em Havering-atte-Bower, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 1356, em Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, com 44 anos, e foi sepultada em Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel le Despenser родилась в 1312 г. в местности глоусестир, глоусестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Eleanor de Clare, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundel 9 февраля 1321 г. в местности хавиринг-атте-боуир, эссекс, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1356 г. в местности глоусестиршир, Англия в возрасте 44 лет и была похоронена в местности тюкесбури эбби, тюкесбури, глоусестиршир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabel le Despenser nació en 1312, en Gloucester, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Eleanor de Clare, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundel el 9 de febrero de 1321, en Havering-atte-Bower, Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1356, en Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 44 años, y fue sepultada en Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.
